items.description: This Order enables the Penwith District Council to—

items.title: The Right to Purchase (Loan Application) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1993
The Right to Purchase (Application Form) (Scotland) Order 1993
items.description: This Order revokes the Right to Purchase (Application Form) (Scotland) Order 1986 and prescribes another form of notice which is to be used by any secure tenant within the meaning of Part III of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987, when exercising the right to purchase his dwellinghouse conferred on him by section 61 of that Act. The notice in this Order has been redesigned and takes account of amendments made to the “right to buy” provisions of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 by the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 principally in respect of the rent to loan scheme and the periods of occupation. The notice contains–

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Assessment Arrangements for English, Welsh, Mathematics and Science) (Key Stage 1) (Wales) Order 1993
items.description: Section 4(1) and (2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying by order appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the subjects.

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Assessment Arrangements for English, Welsh, Mathematics and Science) (Key Stage 3) (Wales) Order 1993
items.description: Section 4(1) and (2) of the Education Reform Act 1988 places a duty on the Secretary of State to establish the National Curriculum by specifying by order appropriate attainment targets, programmes of study and assessment arrangements for each of the foundation subjects. Attainment targets and programmes of study for pupils in the third key stage in the subjects of English, Welsh, mathematics and science have been specified by the Orders referred to in the second footnote to article 3.

items.title: The Eastwood and East Kilbride Districts (Busby) Boundaries Amendment (No.2) Order 1993
items.description: This Order transfers on 1st April 1994 an area of approximately 166.095 hectares at Busby from East Kilbride District to Eastwood District both in Strathclyde Region and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Education (School Performance Information) (Wales) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations, made under Sections 22 and 232(5) and (6) of the Education Reform Act 1988 and Sections 16 and 19(3) of the Education Reform Act 1992 apply to schools in Wales (regulation 1)

items.title: The National Curriculum Council and School Examinations and Assessment Council (Transfer of Property) Order 1993
The Motor Vehicles (EC Type Approval) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1993
The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Miscellaneous Provisions) (England) Order 1993
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential upon and supplementary to the Local Government Finance Act 1992. It extends only to England.

items.title: The Superannuation (Children’s Pensions) (Earnings Limit) Order 1993
The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval for Goods Vehicles) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval for Goods Vehicles) (Great Britain) Regulations 1982.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
The Bananas (Interim Measures) (Revocation) Regulations 1993
The Wireless Telegraphy (Television Licence Fees) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1993
The National Health Service (General Dental Services) Amendment Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) Regulations 1992 by providing that an FHSA shall not include any dentist in its dental list unless satisfied that he has completed a period of vocational training, or has acquired dental experience or training which may be regarded as the equivalent of such training, or is within one of the specified categories of exemption from the vocational training requirements. The FHSA has the function of allocating a vocational training number to an applicant where it is so satisfied and the regulations provide for a right of appeal and for appeal procedures in cases where the FHSA determines not to allocate a vocational training number. The right of appeal is to the body specified in and set up by these Regulations. The functions of FHSAs under these Regulations are all to be exercised by a Special Health Authority called the Dental Vocational Training Authority.

items.title: The Insolvency Practitioners (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insolvency Practitioners Regulations 1990 with effect from 1st April 1993. They amend the prescribed requirements in respect of security or caution for the proper performance of the functions of an insolvency practitioner where he is appointed to act in relation to a person on or after that date, except for certain savings, and the records in respect of such security or caution to be kept for inspection or submitted to his authorising body by an insolvency practitioner in respect of the estate of each person in relation to whom the practitioner acts.

items.title: The Dental Vocational Training Authority Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations provide rules to govern the membership and procedure of the Dental Vocational Training Authority. This is a special health authority established by Order and which is to carry out, on behalf of Family Health Services Authorities in England and Wales their functions in respect of vocational training for dentists applying to be included in a dental list.

items.title: The Dental Vocational Training Authority (Establishment and Constitution) and Appeal Body (Specification) Order 1993
items.description: This Order provides for the establishment and constitution of a special health authority, to be known as the Dental Vocational Training Authority (“the Authority”), to exercise certain functions on behalf of Family Health Services Authorities in England and Wales in connection with general dental services under the national health service. It also recognises the Appeal Body established to deal with appeals from the Authority as a body within section 102 of the National Health Service Act 1977 which allows the members of the Appeal Body to be paid travelling and attendance expenses and remuneration for loss of time.

items.title: The Taxes (Interest Rate) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the Taxes (Interest Rate) Regulations 1989 (S.I. 1989/1297). They insert two additional regulations which specify the interest rate applicable under section 178 of the Finance Act 1989 in relation to overdue corporation tax and to tax overpaid (section 87A of the Taxes Management Act 1970 and section 826 of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 respectively). The additional inserted regulations also make provision for changes in the interest rate applicable, and for the formula to be used in calculating the new rate.

items.title: The Friendly Societies Act 1992 (Commencement No.6 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1993
items.description: Article 2 of this Order brings into force for all remaining purposes the provisions of the Friendly Societies Act 1992 listed in Schedules 2 and 5 on 13th September 1993 and 1st January 1994 respectively, brings into force generally the provisions of the Act listed in Schedules 3 and 6 on 13th September 1993 and 1st January 1994 respectively and brings into force the provisions of the Act listed in Schedule 4 for specified purposes on 13th September 1993.The provisions listed in Schedule 2 concern the management and administration of friendly societies and the regulation of their business.The provisions listed in Schedule 3 concern amalgamations, transfers of engagements and the conversion of friendly societies into companies.Schedule 4 lists various regulation-making powers and Schedule 5 lists provisions on the appointment of actuaries, investigations into the financial position of friendly societies and accounts and audit.Schedule 6 comprises a number of miscellaneous provisions.The provisions of Schedules 2 and 5 were brought into force for friendly societies incorporated under the 1992 Act by the Friendly Societies Act 1992 (Commencement No.3 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/16) (C.1) and will now come into force for friendly societies registered under the Friendly Societies Act 1974.Article 2 also brings into force section 31 of the Act which requires friendly societies to be authorised to carry on business in the UK in relation to registered friendly societies whose specified income determined in accordance with Schedule 1 to the Order exceeded £3,000 for 1991 or for the last preceding year for which an annual return was submitted.Section 31 is brought into force on 1st January 1994 for such societies which do not apply for authorisation before that date and is also brought into force on that date in respect of the insurance business for which a society is deemed to be authorised under section 32(7).Section 31 is brought into force on 1st July 1994 for such societies which do apply for authorisation before 1st January 1994.

items.title: The Finance Act 1993 (Appointed Day) Order 1993
The Finance Act 1993, section 12, (Appointed Day) Order 1993
The National Health Service (District Health Authorities) (No.2) Order 1993
items.description: This Order abolishes on 1st October 1993 the District Health Authorities specified in Schedule 1 (article 2), and establishes the new District Health Authorities specified in Schedule 2 in relation to the districts for which they are established (article 3). The National Health Service (District Health Authorities) Order 1990 is amended to reflect these changes (article 4).

items.title: The National Health Service (Determination of Districts) (No.2) Order 1993
items.description: This Order abolishes on 1st October 1993 the districts of Crewe, East Berkshire, Halton, Macclesfield, Southport and Formby, South Sefton (Merseyside), Warrington and West Berkshire, determines new districts and varies other districts, for the purposes of the National Health Service Act 1977 (article 2).

items.title: The Taxes (Interest Rate) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
The National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 by providing that a Health Board shall not include any dentist in its dental list unless he has a vocational training number. A vocational training number will be allocated to an applicant dentist by a Special Health Board called the Scottish Council for Postgraduate Medical and Dental Education where the Council is satisfied that the applicant has completed a period of vocational training, or has acquired clinical dental experience or training which may be regarded as the equivalent of such training, or is within one of the specified categories of exemption from the vocational training requirements. The Regulations provide for a right of appeal and appeal procedures in a case where the Council determines not to allocate a vocational training number. The right of appeal is to the body specified in and set up by these Regulations.

items.title: The Parole Board (Scotland) Rules 1993
items.description: These Rules make provision with respect to the proceedings of the Parole Board for Scotland (“the Board”), in dealing with cases relating to a person (“the person concerned”) under Part I of the Prisoners and Criminal Proceedings (Scotland) Act 1993 (“the Act”) which are specified in Rule 2(2).

items.title: The Transcripts of Criminal Proceedings (Scotland) Order 1993
items.description: This Order makes provision for restrictions on the availability under section 275 of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1975 of transcripts of proceedings.

items.title: The Prison (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1993
items.description: These Rules make various amendments to the Prison (Scotland) Rules 1952.

items.title: The Young Offenders (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1993

items.title: The National Curriculum Council and School Examinations and Assessment Council (Designation of Staff) Order 1993
items.description: This Order concerns certain staff of the National Curriculum Council and the School Examinations and Assessment Council established under the Education Reform Act 1988. These bodies are to be dissolved under section 246 of the Education Act 1993 in consequence of the establishment (under section 244 of that Act) of the School Curriculum and Assessment Authority. Under section 248 of the Education Act 1993, the Secretary of State may designate (either individually or by class or description) employees of the existing bodies for automatic transfer to the new Authority. Where an individual is so designated there is no termination of, or change in the terms of, his contract of employment. This Order lists those staff so designated.

It also makes consequential modifications to the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 and to Part II of the 1991 Act and amends the Road Traffic (Special Parking Areas) (The London Borough of Wandsworth) Order 1993.

items.title: The District Electoral Areas (Northern Ireland) Order 1993
items.description: Article 2(3) of the Local Government (Boundaries) Order (Northern Ireland) 1992 constitutes new wards for each local government district in Northern Ireland. Consequent on the making of that Order, the Secretary of State appointed a Commissioner under article 3(1) of the District Electoral Areas Commissioner (Northern Ireland) Order 1984 (S.I. 1984/360). Under article 3(2) of that Order, the Commissioner was required to submit a report setting out, in respect of each district, his recommenda tions for the grouping together of those wards into electoral areas for the purpose of local government elections in each of those districts. This Order gives effect, without modification, to the recommendations in the Report by the District Electoral Areas Commissioner which was laid before Parliament under article 5 of the District Electoral Areas Commissioner (Northern Ireland) Order 1984 on 10th December 1992.

items.title: The Hydrocarbon Oil (Amendment) Regulations 1993
The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Horsham District Council and Worthing Borough Council) Order 1993
The Parliamentary Constituencies (Wales) (Miscellaneous Changes) Order 1993
items.description: This Order gives effect without modifications to the recommendations contained in the report of the Boundary Commission for Wales dated 30th September 1992. The report contains proposals for changes to the areas of certain parliamentary constituencies where county boundary changes have resulted in inconsistencies between those boundaries and the boundaries of parliamentary constituencies. Article 2 of, and the Schedule to, this Order gives effect to those proposals (together with the new names for the Ceredigion Gogledd Penfro and Montgomeryshire constituencies). Article 3 of this Order gives effect to the proposal that the name of the constituency formerly known as Brecon and Radnor be changed. By virtue of section 4(6) of the Parliamentary Constituencies Act 1986, the coming into force of this Order does not affect any parliamentary election until a proclamation is issued by Her Majesty summoning a new Parliament, or affect the constitution of the House of Commons until the dissolution of the Parliament then in being.

items.title: The Finance (No. 2) Act 1992 (Commencement No. 6 and Transitional Provisions and Savings) Order 1993
items.description: This Order brings into force on 13th October 1993 the repeals effected by section 12 of the Finance (No.2) Act 1992 of section 7 of the Finance Act 1971 and section 7(2C) and (2D) of the Vehicles (Excise) Act 1971 (corresponding Northern Ireland provision) which exempt from vehicles excise duty certain vehicles which are used for the purposes of disabled people.Transitional provisions and savings are made to preserve the exemption from duty in certain cases.

items.title: The Income Tax (Employments) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Employments) Regulations 1993 (S.I.1993/744) (“the principal Regulations”).

The Academic Awards and Distinctions (Queen Margaret College) (Scotland) Order of Council 1993
items.description: The Academic Awards and Distinctions (Queen Margaret College) (Scotland) Order of Council 1992 specified Queen Margaret College as competent to grant degrees, diplomas, certificates or other academic awards or distinctions with the specific exception of post-graduate degrees.  This Order replaces the earlier order and extends the relevant powers to cover post-graduate awards for taught courses.  Post graduate research degrees continue to be excluded.  Other academic awards and distinctions granted on completion of programmesof supervised research are also now excluded.

items.title: The Sandwell Borough Council (Patent Shaft/Moorcroft Infrastructure) (Walsall Canal Bridge) Scheme 1993 Confirmation Instrument 1993
The Air Navigation (Third Amendment) Order 1993
items.description: This Order amends the Air Navigation Order 1989.

items.title: The Local Government Finance Act 1992 (Community Charge Benefit) Savings and Transitional Order 1993
items.description: This Order provides that, notwithstanding amendments made to sections 140 and 163(2)(d) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (community charge benefit subsidy) by Schedule 9 of the Local Government Finance Act 1992, which replaced references to community charge benefit subsidy by references to council tax benefit subsidy, the Secretary of State may exercise the power to make provision in respect of community charge benefit subsidy in relation to any year which ends before 1st April 1993, as if the amendments referred to had not been made. It also provides that the Secretary of State may withhold adjust or deduct amounts from payments of council tax benefit subsidy which are due to a billing or levying authority for the purpose of settling claims by that authority for community charge benefit subsidy.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Canadian Navigation Services) (Fourth Amendment) Regulations 1993
The Highlands and Islands Rural Enterprise Programme (Revocation) Regulations 1993
The Value Added Tax (Reverse Charge) Order 1993
items.description: This Order varies, with effect from 1st November 1993, Schedule 3 to the Value Added Tax Act 1983.Article 3 substitutes a new paragraph 8 of that Schedule extending the application of the reverse charge provisions of section 7 of that Act to all supplies (which are treated as made in the United Kingdom) of services not previously covered where the customer is registered in the United Kingdom.The services specifed in the existing paragraph 8 are subsumed in the new provision, so the 1992 Order of the same title (which added that paragraph) becomes spent and is revoked accordingly by Article 5. Article 4 provides that section 7 of the Act will apply to these services even if the recipient would not “belong” in the United Kingdom if the rules in section 8 of the Act were applied.

items.title: The Funds for Trade Union Ballots Regulations (Revocation) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations come into force on 1st April 1993 and, with effect from 1st April 1996, abolish the scheme under which, on application, the Certification Officer refunds independent trade unions in respect of certain expenditure incurred by them in conducting certain ballots. The Regulations also (i) progressively reduce the proportion of that expenditure which is to be refunded in respect of ballots conducted during the intervening period and (ii) ensure that no refund is to be made if the Certification Officer considers that a statutory requirement applying to the conduct of a ballot has not been satisfied.

items.title: The Telecommunications (Leased Lines) Regulations 1993
items.description: Council Directive 92/44/EEC on the application of open network provision to leased lines (“the Leased Lines Directive”) harmonizes the technical characteristics for leased lines on the public telecommunication network, and provides for certain requirements in relation to access to leased lines and to the availability of information about the conditions of supply of such lines.

The Combined Probation Areas (East Sussex) Order 1993
The Non-Domestic Rates (Levying) (Scotland) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the rateable value in accordance with which the non-domestic rate will be levied for the financial years 1993-94 and 1994-95.

items.title: The Plant Health Fees (Scotland) Amendment Order 1993
items.description: This Order corrects an error in the Plant Health Fees (Scotland) Order 1993 which referred to article 14 of the Plant Health (Great Britain) Order 1993 (S.I. 1993/1320) instead of article 17 of that Order.

They also make certain other minor amendments.

The Angola (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1993
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No 864 of15th September 1993 on the export to Angola and the supply to persons in Angola of arms and related mate riel, and petroleum and petroleum products. It restricts certain related activities including the carriage of prohibited goods in ships, aircraft and vehicles.

items.title: The Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) Order 1993
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, applies to each of the territories specified in Schedule 3. It imposes restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No 864 of 15th September 1993, on the export to Angola and the supply to persons in Angola of arms and related mate riel, and petroleum and petroleum products. It restricts certain related activities including the carriage of prohibited goods in ships, aircraft and vehicles.

items.title: The Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 1993
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, imposes in the Channel Islands restrictions pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 864 of 15th September 1993, on the export to Angola and the supply to persons in Angola of arms and related material, and petroleum and petroleum products. It restricts certain related activities including the carriage of prohibited goods in ships, aircraft and vehicles.

items.title: The Angola (United Nations Sanctions) (Isle of Man) Order 1993
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, gives effect in the Isle of Man to certain provisions of a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 864 of 15th September 1993, on the export to Angola and the supply to persons in Angola on arms and related matériel, and petroleum and petroleum products. It restricts certain related activities including the carriage of prohibited goods in ships, aircraft and vehicles.

items.description: These Regulations, which apply to the United Kingdom, implement Council Directive No. 76/764/EEC, as amended, relating to clinical mercury–in–glass, maximum reading thermometers. They apply to clinical thermometers designed to measure the internal temperature of humans or animals (regulation 3).

items.description: Under Part I of the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”), leasehold valuation tribunals have jurisdiction to determine the terms of acquisition of interests (whether by enfranchisement under Chapter I or by the grant of a new lease under Chapter II) where these are in dispute, and to approve estate management schemes under Chapter IV. Section 91 of the Act provides that this jurisdiction, and additional jurisdiction to determine certain related matters, is to be exercised by rent assessment committees acting as leasehold valuation tribunals.

items.title: The Leasehold Reform (Notices) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the forms in the Appendix to the Leasehold Reform (Notices) Regulations 1967 (forms of notice to be used by tenants of houses claiming the right to have the freehold or an extended lease under the Leasehold Reform Act 1967). The amendments are consequential upon changes made to the Leasehold Reform Act 1967 by the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993.

items.title: The Local Government and Housing Act 1989 (Commencement No. 16) Order 1993
items.description: Article 3 of this Order brings into force on 7th October 1993 the provisions in sections 67 to 70 and section 72, in so far as they are not already in force, and section 73, of the Local Government and Housing Act 1989.These provisions relate to companies in which local authorities have interests, and trusts which are subject to local authority influence.

items.title: The Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Amendment Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 1A of the Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Regulations 1988 so that persons whose applicable amount for the purposes of income support includes a residential allowance, shall not be entitled to a cold weather payment out of the Social Fund. They amend Schedule 1 to those Regulations making changes to the areas linked to national climatological message stations by way of Post Office postcodes and also make additions to Table 1 in Schedule 2 to those Regulations which identifies postcode areas by reference to maps produced by Datamaps Ltd.

The Restrictive Trade Practices (Standards and Arrangements) (Services) Order 1993
items.description: This Order approves standards of performance in the provision of services, and standards of dimension, design, quality or performance in respect of goods used in providing them and an arrangement as to the provision of information or advice, all of which have been adopted by Electricity Association Services Limited. The effect of the Order is that, for the purpose of determining whether an agreement is one to which the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 applies, no account is taken of any term in the agreement by which the parties or any of them agree to comply with, or to apply, the standards and arrangement so approved.

items.title: The Road Traffic Accidents (Payments for Treatment) Order 1993
items.description: This Order increases the amounts payable under sections 157(2) and 158(2) of the Road Traffic Act 1988. Section 157 provides for payments for hospital treatment of traffic casualties to be made by an owner or insurer of a vehicle the use of which gave rise to the injuries, and section 158 for payments to be made, by the person using such a vehicle, for emergency treatment and a medical practitioner’s travelling expenses.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest Relief) (Qualifying Lenders) (No. 3) Order 1993
items.description: A person who is a qualifying borrower, and who makes a payment of relevant loan interest to a qualifying lender, is entitled to pay that interest under deduction of tax under the mortgage interest relief at source scheme (MIRAS).

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 4) Order 1993
The Miscellaneous Factories (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1993
The Design Right (Semiconductor Topographies) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations replace the Schedule to the Design Right (Semiconductor Topographies) Regulations 1989 with two new Parts to show the current position on the additional classes of qualifying persons enjoying legal protection of topographies of semiconductor products. The addition of Liechtenstein to the Part II countries gives effect to Council Decision 90/511/EEC (O.J. No. L285, 17.10.1990, page 31). The United States of America is included in that Part by virtue of Council Decision 93/16/ EEC (O.J. No. L11, 19.1.93, page 20), and Commission Decision 93/217/EEC (O.J. No. L94, 20.4.93, page 30). Iceland and Norway, which were shown under Part III of the earlier Schedule (which related to citizens, subjects and habitual residents only of the countries specified) are now shown under Part II by virtue of Council Decision 93/17/ EEC (O.J. No. L11, 19.1.93, page 22). With these changes, no countries remain in Part III of the earlier Schedule and accordingly that Part is omitted from the new Schedule.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Beverages) Order 1993
The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
The Lanarkshire (Monklands) Enterprise Zones Designation Order 1993
The Trade Union Reform and Employment Rights Act 1993 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1993
items.description: This Order brings into force provisions of the Trade Union Reform and Employment Rights Act 1993 as follows—

items.title: The Police (Promotion) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1993
The Combined Probation Areas (Dorset) Order 1993
The Teachers' (Superannuation and Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Teachers' Superannuation (Scotland) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”) and the Teachers' (Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Regulations 1980 (“the compensation Regulations”).

items.title: The Coal Industry Act 1992 (Commencement) Order 1993
items.description: This Order brings into force on 20th November 1993 the provisions of section 2 of the Coal Industry Act 1992 (“the Act”), Part II of the Schedule to the Act, and so much of section 3(3) of the Act as relates to Part II of the Schedule to the Act; these provisions repeal the Coal Mines Regulation Act 1908 (c. 57) and make certain other repeals which are consequential on the repeal of that Act. The Act and, therefore, the repeals made by the Act do not extend to Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment No. 5) Order 1993
items.description: This Order amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1992:

items.title: The Friendly Societies (Qualifications of Actuaries No.2) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the qualifications to be held by actuaries appointed by friendly societies in accordance with section 44 of the Friendly Societies Act 1992. The Regulations revoke the Friendly Societies (Qualifications of Actuaries) Regulations 1993 (S.I.993/60) which prescribed the qualifications for actuaries of incorporated friendly societies. These Regulations re-enact those provisions so as to prescribe the same qualifications for actuaries of incorporated friendly societies and registered friendly societies subject to one modification. An actuary who is a Fellow of the Institute of Actuaries or of the Faculty of Actuaries but who has not yet attained the age of 30 years may be appointed as actuary to a registered friendly society if he carried out the last valuation of the society under section 41 of the Friendly Societies Act 1974 prior to entry into force of the Regulations.

items.title: The Friendly Societies (Amendment) Regulations 1993 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which make amendments to the Friendly Societies Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”) and the Contracts (Applicable Law) Act 1990, implement—

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (Wales) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations provide for the contents of rate demand notices, which are issued by billing authorities (district councils) in Wales, and for the information to be supplied when such notices are served by them.

items.title: The Friendly Societies (Insurance Business No. 2) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations revoke–

items.title: The Friendly Societies (Authorisation No. 2) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Friendly Societies (Authorisation) Regulations 1993 (S. I.1993/99) and re-enact those provisions so as to prescribe the information which an incorporated or a registered friendly society is required to provide to the Friendly Societies Commission (“the Commission”) when applying for authorisation under section 32 of the Friendly Societies Act 1992 (“the Act”). These Regulations do not apply to applications under section 33 of the Act.

items.title: The A11 Trunk Road (Roudham Heath to Attleborough Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1993
The A11 Trunk Road (Roudham Heath to Attleborough Improvement) (Detrunking) Order 1993
The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 2) Order 1993
The Police (Amendment) (No. 4) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police Regulations 1987. Regulation 2 increases, with effect from 1st April 1993, the maximum amount of removal allowance payable in respect of incidental expenses. Regulation 3 increases pay, and regulation 4 increases the dog handler’s allowance, both with effect from 1st September 1993. Retrospection is authorised by section 33(4) of the Police Act 1964.

items.title: The Police Cadets (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police Cadets Regulations 1979. Regulation 2 increases pay from 1st September 1992 and again from 1st September 1993 (retrospection is authorised by section 33(4), as applied by section 35(2), of the Police Act 1964). Regulation 3 increases charges payable by cadets for board and lodging provided by police authorities.

items.title: The Fodder Plant Seeds (Amendment) Regulations 1993
The Council Tax (Transitional Reduction Scheme) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend Part II of Schedule 2 to the Council Tax (Transitional Reduction Scheme) (England) Regulations 1993.

items.title: The Seeds (Registration, Licensing and Enforcement) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
The Local Government Superannuation (Maternity Absence) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government Superannuation Regulations 1986. They provide for the contributions and remuneration for pension purposes of women on maternity leave. These Regulations implement in respect of the local government superannuation scheme and maternity leave the requirements of the Directive on the implementation of the principle of equal treatment for men and women in occupational social security schemes (86/378/EEC; OJ No. L225, 12.8.86, p. 40). Regulation 2 is by virtue of section 12 of the Superannuation Act 1972 made retrospective to 1st January 1993. Regulation 3 confers a right for a person, in certain circumstances, to opt that these Regulations shall not apply to her.

items.title: The Sea Fisheries Districts (Variation) Order 1993
items.description: This Order varies the provisions of the Sea Fisheries District Orders for the Cornwall, Cumbria, Devon, Eastern, Kent and Essex, North–Eastern, Northumberland, North Western and North Wales, Southern and South Wales Sea Fisheries Districts by providing that the district shall comprise so much of the sea as is within six miles of the baselines (formerly three miles).

items.title: The Suppression of Terrorism Act 1978 (Application of Provisions) (India) Order 1993
The Medicines (Applications for Grant of Product Licences—Products for Human Use) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the information, documents, samples and other material to be provided in or with an application, made after the coming into force of the Regulations, for a product licence under Part II of the Medicines Act 1968 (“the Act”) in relation to medicinal products for human use. They replace, in relation to such applications, the requirements of the Medicines (Applications for Product Licences and Clinical Trial and Animal Test Certificates) Regulations 1971 (S.I. 1971/973) which are amended in consequence.

items.title: The Medicines (Standard Provisions for Licences and Certificates) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1993
The Rules of the Air (Amendment) Regulations 1993
The Birmingham Women’s Health Care National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1993
items.description: This Order establishes the Birmingham Women’s Health Care National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Salmon (Definition of Methods of Net Fishing and Construction of Nets) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend Regulation 2 of the Salmon (Definition of Methods of Net Fishing and Construction of Nets) (Scotland) Regulations 1992. Adjustments are made to the definition of fishing by net and coble or by bag net, fly net or other stake net to cover working the net from bank or shore or adjacent to bank or shore and to prevent the use of nets or traps designed or constructed to enmesh the salmon.  These Regulations also amend Regulations 8 and 9 to make it clear that they do not impinge on the operation of Regulation 2.

It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Hill Livestock (Compensatory Allowances) Regulations 1992, as amended. The Regulations extend to Great Britain. They comply with Council Directive 75/268/EEC on mountain and hill farming and farming in certain less–favoured areas, as amended by Council Directive No. 80/666/ EEC, and with Articles 17 to 19 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2328/91 on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 870/93. The Regulations also make provision for the first time for the administration and enforcement of provisions for payment of compensatory allowances contained in Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 3887/92 laying down detailed rules for applying the integrated administration and control system for certain Community aid schemes.

items.title: The National Lottery etc. Act 1993 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1993
items.description: This Order brings into force—

items.title: The South East London Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1993
items.description: This Order establishes the South East London Mental Health National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and also the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 7).

items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals of the Construction Industry Training Board which were submitted to the Secretary of State.  The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the construction industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Engineering Construction Board) Order 1993
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals of the Engineering Construction Industry Training Board which were submitted to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the engineering construction industry (including employers who are not mainly engaged in engineering construction activities but are engaged in some such activities and for those employers the levy will be imposed only in respect of their engineering construction activities) for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Private Legislation Procedure (Scotland) General Order 1993
items.description: This Order amends the General Orders made under section 15 of the Private Legislation Procedure (Scotland) Act 1936, which regulate the procedure for obtaining Parliamentary powers by way of Provisional Order in matters affecting Scotland.

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) ( No. 3) Order 1993 (revoked)
The Birmingham City Council (Grand Union Canal Bridge) Scheme 1991 Confirmation Instrument 1993
The European Convention on Extradition (Fiscal Offences) Order 1993
items.description: This Order gives effect to Chapter II of the Second Additional Protocol to the European Convention on Extradition which the United Kingdom is to ratify. Chapter II substitutes a new Article 5 of the Convention which removes the existing restriction on extradition under the Convention for fiscal offences. Extradition for such an offence will be able to take place between Contracting Parties which have accepted Chapter II, in accordance with the Convention, if the offence corresponds to an offence of the same nature under the law of the requested Party.

items.title: The Admiralty Jurisdiction (Guernsey) Order 1993
The Arms Control and Disarmament (Privileges and Immunities) Act 1988 (Guernsey) Order 1993
The European Convention on Extradition (Hungary and Poland) (Amendment) Order 1993 (revoked)
The Treaty on Open Skies (Privileges and Immunities) (Guernsey) Order 1993
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities upon persons designated by States Parties to the Treaty on Open Skies, signed at Helsinki on the twenty-fourth of March 1992 (Cm 2067), while they are in the Bailiwick of Guernsey for the purpose of carrying out duties relating to the conduct of overflights pursuant to that Treaty.

The Regulations contain, in Schedules 1 to 5, new rules of procedure for proceedings before industrial tribunals which replace the existing rules of procedure. The Regulations apply to proceedings in England and Wales.

items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 16 December 1993, replace the regulations which establish industrial tribunals. items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Introduction of Disability Living Allowance) Regulations 1991 by—

items.title: The Capital Allowances (Corresponding Northern Ireland Grants) Order 1993
items.description: This Order specifies certain grants made in Northern Ireland under the Industrial Development (Northern Ireland) Order 1982 as corresponding to grants made in Great Britain under Part II of the Industrial Development Act 1982 and is in succession to the Capital Allowances (Corresponding Northern Ireland Grants) Order 1991 (S. I.1991/518), which applies to grants made under agreements entered into before 1st April 1993. The effect of the Order is that the amounts of the specified grants are not deducted from the recipient’s capital expenditure when his capital allowances are calculated.(Grants under Part II of the Industrial Development Act 1982 are not made in Great Britain after 31st March 1988 except where application for approval of the project was received by the Secretary of State on or before 31st March 1988, see section 1 of the Regional Development Grants (Termination) Act 1988 (c. 11).)

items.title: The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) (No. 3) Order 1993
items.description: The principal effect of this Order is to consolidate the Orders revoked by article 5 and to make amendments to those Orders. Article 3 designates Girobank and various types of corporate body (specified in Part I of the Schedule) which a building society may invest in and support or support under section 18 of the Building Societies Act 1986. In addition to minor and drafting amendments, this Order makes changes of substance as set out below.

items.title: The Education (No. 2) Act 1986 (Amendment) Order 1993
The St Austell College (Government) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the form of instrument of government and articles of government for St Austell College.

items.title: The Housing Renovation etc. Grants (Grant Limit) (Amendment) Order 1993
The Reconstitution of the River Ouzel Internal Drainage Board Order 1993
The Placing on the Market and Supervision of Transfers of Explosives Regulations 1993
The Hereford and Worcester and Warwickshire (County Boundaries) Order 1993
The Housing and Planning Act 1986 (Commencement No. 18 and Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Order 1993
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 35 to 38 of and Schedule 7, Part II to the Housing and Planning Act 1986 (“the 1986 Act”) and applies only to Scotland.

items.title: The Food Labelling (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1993
The Teachers (Education, Training and Recommendation for Registration) (Scotland) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the admission of students to courses of education and training leading to teaching qualifications in Scotland, as to the requirements which such courses must satisfy, as to the teaching qualifications which may be awarded, and as to the recommendation of persons so qualified for registration by the General Teaching Council for Scotland (“the Council”). They supersede the Teachers (Education, Training and Regis-tration) (Scotland) Regulations 1967.

items.title: The Portsmouth Mile End Quay (Berth No. 2 Extension) Harbour Revision Order 1993
items.description: This Order empowers Portsmouth City Council as the harbour authority for the Mile End Quay to construct a quay and to reclaim land to the south and west of the existing Portsmouth Continental Ferry Port Berth Number 2.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1993 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1993
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st December 1993 the provisions of the Criminal Justice Act 1993 specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.description: These Regulations—

items.title: The Environmental Protection Act 1990 (Commencement No. 13) Order 1993
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”).

items.title: The Planning and Compensation Act 1991 (Commencement No. 15) (Scotland) Order 1993
The Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Operators' Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles (Operators' Licences) Regulations 1986, which consolidated with modifications the Public Service Vehicles (Operators' Licences) Regulations 1981.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Traffic Commissioners: Publication and Inquiries) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles (Traffic Commissioners: Publication and Inquiries) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Copyright (Certification of Licensing Scheme for Educational Recording of Broadcasts) (Open University Educational Enterprises Limited) Order 1993
items.description: Under section 35 of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 recordings of broadcasts and cable programmes may be made by or on behalf of educational establishments without thereby infringing copyright. The section does not apply, however, if and to the extent that there is a licensing scheme certified for the purposes of that section providing for the grant of licences.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Priority of Charges) (No. 2) Order 1993
The Mortgage Indemnities (Recognised Bodies) (No.2) Order 1993
The Food Labelling (Amendment) Regulations 1993
The River Tay Catchment Area Protection (Renewal) Order 1993
items.description: This Order renews for a second time the prohibition contained in the River Tay Catchment Area Protection Order 1986 which prohibited fishing for or taking freshwater fish in inland waters in the area prescribed in that Order without legal right or without written permission from someone having such a right. The effect of the renewal is that the prohibition applies during the period from 22nd February 1993 to 21st February 1996.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment No. 2) 1993
The Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1991 (Codes of Practice) (No. 1) Order 1993
The Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 (Commencement and Transitional Provisions No. 3) Order 1993
items.description: Article 3 of this Order brings the following provisions of the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 into force on 10th November 1993—

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Central Borders) Designation Order 1993
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Stewartry) Designation Order 1993
The Council Tax (Transitional Reduction Scheme) (Scotland) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations provide for the reduction in certain cases of the amount that a person is liable to pay to a levying authority in Scotland by way of council tax. They have effect in relation to the financial year beginning on 1st April 1993 (regulation 3).

items.title: The Charities (Designated Religious Bodies) (Scotland) Order 1993
items.description: This Order designates for the purposes of section 3 of the Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1990 the recognised bodies (or Scottish charities) which appear to the Secretary of State to meet the criteria set out in that section (designated religious bodies).

items.title: The Plant Breeders' Rights (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the Plant Breeders' Rights Regulations 1978.

items.title: The Plant Breeders' Rights (Trees, Shrubs and Woody Climbers) Scheme 1993
items.description: This Scheme supersedes the Plant Breeders' Rights (Trees, Shrubs and Woody Climbers) Scheme 1969 as amended. It prescribes the varieties of trees, shrubs and woody climbers, which belong to the genera or species which are specified in column 1 of the Schedule, in respect of which grants of plant breeders' rights may be made and also prescribes the periods of years, which are specified in column 2 of the Schedule, as those during which the rights may be exercised.

items.title: The Plant Breeders' Rights (Tomatoes) Scheme 1993
The Plant Breeders' Rights (Miscellaneous Ornamental Plants) (Variation) Scheme 1993
The Plant Breeders' Rights (Sainfoin and Birdsfoot Trefoil) Scheme 1993
items.description: This Scheme, made under the Plant Varieties and Seeds Act 1964 prescribes all varieties of Sainfoin and Birdsfoot Trefoil as varieties in respect of which grants of plant breeders' rights may be made and also prescribes the period of twenty-five years as that during which the rights may be exercised (paragraphs 2 and 3).

items.title: The Removal and Disposal of Vehicles (Amendment) Regulations 1993
The Plant Breeders' Rights (Herbaceous Perennials) (Variation) Scheme 1993
The Plant Breeders' Rights (Quince Rootstocks) Scheme 1993
items.description: This Scheme, made under the Plant Varieties and Seeds Act 1964, prescribes the varieties of quince rootstocks in respect of which grants of plant breeders' rights may be made and also prescribes the period of 30 years as that during which the rights may be exercised (pararaphs 2 and 3).

items.title: The Finance Act 1993 (Appointed Day No. 2) Order 1993
The Local Government Superannuation (South Yorkshire Transport Limited) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations make special provision for meeting the cost of pension increases for employees and former employees of South Yorkshire Transport Limited, a company admitted to the superannuation fund administered by the South Yorkshire Pension Authority. The pension increases arise from the provisions of the Pensions (Increase) Act 1971.

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1991 (Codes of Practice) (No. 2) Order 1993
The County Court Appeals (Amendment) (Transitional Provisions) Order 1993
The Guaranteed Minimum Pensions Increase Order 1993
The Transport Act 1985 (Modifications in Schedule 4 to the Transport Act 1968) (Further Modification) Order 1993
items.description: This Order makes a further modification to Schedule 4 to the Transport Act 1968,as modified in its application to transfers under sections 50(4), 59(7), 61(11) and 68(7) of the Transport Act 1985 by the Transport Act 1985 (Modifications in Schedule 4 to the Transport Act 1968) Order 1985. The text of Schedule 4 as so modified is setout in the Second Schedule to that Order. This Order adds a further paragraph to the modified Schedule 4 for the purpose of adapting the provisions of that Schedule to transfer schemes under section 61 of the Transport Act 1985, whereby the undertaking of a public transport company owned by a Passenger Transport Authority is divided between two or more successor companies.

items.title: The Sex Discrimination and Equal Pay (Remedies) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972, are made for the purpose of ensuring that the remedies available under legislation in Great Britain relating to sex discrimination and to equal pay for men and women comply with the requirements of Council Directives 1975/117/EEC and 1976/207/EEC (following the judgment of the European Court of Justice in Case No C271/91-Marshall v Southampton and South-West Hampshire Area Health Authority (No.2)). They come into force on 22nd November 1993.

items.title: The Home Energy Efficiency Grants (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the Home Energy Efficiency Grants Regulations 1992, which provide in particular for the making of grants for the improvement of energy efficiency in dwellings occupied by persons of low income. These Regulations substitute a new regulation 9 (calculation of the size of grant). The maximum amounts of the grants payable in respect of works at such dwellings are increased. Contributions from applicants for grants are no longer specified. The former regulation 9 continues to govern cases where grant was applied for before 9th December 1993.

items.title: The Warrington Hospital National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1993
items.description: This Order establishes the Warrington Hospital National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Re-rating) Order 1993
items.description: This Order increases the amounts of weekly earnings specified in the secondary earnings brackets in section 9(3) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (“the Act”), in respect of Bracket 1 from £89.99 to £94.99, in respect of Bracket 2 from £90.00 and £134.99 to £95.00 and £139.99, in respect of Bracket 3 from £135.00 and £189.99 to £140.00 and £194.99 and in respect of Bracket 4 from £190.00 to £195.00 (article 2).

items.title: The Scottish Electricity Boards (Dissolution) Order 1993
The Road Traffic Act 1991 (Commencement No. 8 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1993
items.description: This Order brings into force in the London boroughs of Camden, Hackney and Hounslow provisions relating to the enforcement of certain parking restrictions.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Special Parking Areas) (London Boroughs of Camden, Hackney and Hounslow) Order 1993
items.description: This Order designates the whole of the London boroughs of Camden, Hackney and Hounslow, excluding the roads and areas referred to in Schedules 1, 2 and 3 respectively, as special parking areas for the purposes of Part II of the Road Traffic Act 1991 with effect from 6th December 1993. The roads in the London borough of Hounslow referred to in Part III of Schedule 3 become a special parking area with effect from 9th January 1994. The Order also makes consequential modifications to the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 and to Part II of the 1991 Act.

items.title: The Meldon Quarry Branch Line Order 1993
items.description: This Order has the effect of transferring from the British Railways Board to Euston Holdings Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Board, certain rights and liabilities conferred or imposed by the Okehampton Railway Acts of 1862 and 1864 and the Devon and Cornwall Railway Act 1868, so far as such rights and liabilities relate o the lands described in article 2 of the Order.

items.title: The Foreign Compensation (Financial Provisions) (No. 2) Order 1993
The Libya (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1993
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, re-enacts, with certain modifications, the provisions of the Libya (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/975) and imposes further restrictions, pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 883 of 11th November 1993, on the export to Libya and the supply to persons connected with Libya of other goods including certain oil industry equipment and airfield infrastructure. It restricts certain activities including the training of Libyan pilots and other aviation personnel. The Order also places restrictions on certain actions making available or otherwise transferring certain funds or other financial resources to or for the benefit of certain persons connected with Libya.

items.title: The Libya (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) Order 1993
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, applies to each of the territories specified in Schedule 3. The Order re-enacts, with certain modifications, the provisions of the Libya (United Nations Sanctions) (Dependent Territories) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/976) and imposes restrictions, pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 883 of 11th November 1993, on the export to Libya and the supply to persons connected with Libya, of further goods, including certain oil industry equipment and airfield infrastructure. It restricts certain activities including the training of Libyan pilots and other aviation personnel. The Order also places restrictions on certain actions making available or otherwise transferring certain funds or other financial resources to or for the benefit of certain persons connected with Libya.

items.title: The State Immunity (Federal States) Order 1993
The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Libya (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 1993
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, re-enacts, with certain modifications, the provisions of the Libya (United Nations Sanctions) (Channel Islands) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/977), and imposes in the Channel Islands further restrictions, pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 883 of 11th November 1993, on the export to Libya and supply to persons connected with Libya of other goods including certain oil industry equipment and airfield infrastructure.

items.title: The Libya (United Nations Sanctions) (Isle of Man) Order 1993
items.description: This Order, made under the United Nations Act 1946, re-enacts, with modifications and for the Isle of Man only, the provisions of the Libya (United Nations Sanctions) Order 1992 (S.I. 1992/975), which extended to the United Kingdom and the Isle of Man. It imposes in the Isle of Man further restrictions, pursuant to a decision of the Security Council of the United Nations in Resolution No. 883 of 11th November 1993, on the export to Libya and supply to persons connected with Libya of other goods including certain oil industry equipment and airfield infrastructure.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Wales) Order 1993
The British Railways (Penalty Fares) Act 1989 (Activating No. 11) Order 1993
items.description: This Order gives effect to the penalty fares provisions contained in sections 4 to 9 of the British Railways (Penalty Fares) Act 1989, with respect to the train services specified in Part I of the Schedule, as from 6th December 1993.

items.title: The Central Manchester National Health Service Trust (Change of Name) Order 1993
items.description: This Order changes the name of an NHS trust established by Order under Part I of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990 from the Central Manchester National Health Service Trust (originally known as the Manchester Central Hospitals and Community Care National Health Service Trust) to the Central Manchester Healthcare National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Cleveland Ambulance National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Amendment Order 1993
The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1993
The Education (School Information) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (School Information) (England) Regulations 1993 by requiring additional information to be included in the information to be published by governors of schools maintained by local education authorities in England. The additional information required is details of any special curricular and other arrangements made for particular categories of pupils, including those with statements of special educational needs (regulation 2), the dates of school holidays during the next school year and the times at which each school session begins and ends on a school day (regulation 3).

The Building Societies (Aggregation) Rules 1993
items.description: These Rules, which replace the Building Societies (Aggregation) Rules 1990, set out how certain assets and liabilities of bodies associated with building societies are to be attributed to societies for the purposes of the limits in section 7 (power to raise funds and borrow money and limit on non–retail funds and borrowing), 8 (proportion of liabilities to be in the form of shares) and 20 (commercial asset structure requirements) of the Building Societies Act 1986.

items.title: The Mid Glamorgan Ambulance National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1993
items.description: This Order establishes Mid Glamorgan Ambulance National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Scottish Power Telecommunications Limited) Order 1993
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to ScottishPower Telecommunications Limited a licence on 24th September 1993 under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in that licence in the United Kingdom.A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 30th November 1993.This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, ScottishPower Telecommunications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 29th December 1993.

Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Torch Communications Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force on 29th December 1993.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Alteration of Lists and Appeals) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Non-Domestic Rating (Alteration of Lists and Appeals) Regulations 1990, together with the instruments which amended them, which are numbered S.I. 1990/769, S.I. 1990/1822, S.I. 1990/2025, S.I. 1991/723 and S.I. 1992/611. They are in many respects identical to the Regulations before revocation. Some changes have been made in consequence of the introduction of the council tax by the Local Government Finance Act 1992; others are minor variations to procedure for consistency with the Council Tax (Alterations of Lists and Appeals) Regulations 1993 and the Valuation and Community Charge Tribunals (Amendment) Regulations 1993. There are changes in the order and numbering.

items.title: The Petroleum Revenue Tax (Nomination Scheme for Disposals and Appropriations) (Amendment) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 22nd December 1993, amend the Petroleum Revenue Tax (Nomination Scheme for Disposals and Appropriations) Regulations 1987 (“the principal Regulations”).  The main effect of the amendments is to alter the requirements for oil to be excluded from the nomination scheme established by Schedule 10 to the Finance Act 1987.  For chargeable periods ending after 31 December 1993, participators will be able to notify the Board of Inland Revenue in advance that they expect to satisfy the conditions for exclusion.  Oil forming part of their equity production will then be excluded from the nomination scheme in those chargeable periods unless they fail in the event to satisfy those conditions or they withdraw the notification.

items.title: The Retirement Benefits Schemes (Indexation of Earnings Cap) (No. 2) Order 1993
items.description: Section 590C(1) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the Act”) provides that in arriving at an employee’s final remuneration for the purposes of section 590(3)(a) or (d) of the Act (conditions for approval of retirement benefit schemes), any excess over the permitted maximum figure (“the earnings cap”) for the year of assessment in which the employee’s participation in the scheme ceases shall be disregarded. Section 590C(3) fixed the earnings cap for the years of assessment 1988–89 and 1989–90 at £60,000.

items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (School Inspection) (Wales) (No. 2) Regulations 1993 (“the Regulations”) by substituting new paragraphs for paragraphs (4) and (5) of regulation 7 of the Regulations. The new paragraphs prescribe respectively the period within which the appropriate authority are to distribute copies of action plans prepared by them following a school inspection and the circumstances in which, and persons to whom, copies of such plans are to be sent. This is in addition to the circumstances and persons specified in paragraph 10(3) and (4) of Schedule 2 to the Education (Schools) Act 1992 and section 210(3) to (5) of the Education Act 1993) (regulation 2(c) and (d)).

items.title: The Inverclyde Royal National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1993

It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by the appropriate health board before its operational date (article 7).

items.description: The Trade Marks and Service Marks (Fees) Rules 1992 (S.I. 1992/1069) require the payment of a fee of 20 in relation to an application for conversion of a specification from the old (pre-1938) classification in Schedule 3 to the Trade Marks and Service Marks Rules 1986 to the new classification in Schedule 4 thereto. The Patent Office is desirous of encouraging more applications for conversions by the abolition of the fee so as to update its register, and these Rules abolish that fee.

The directive is within the scope of the Agreement on the European Economic Area by virtue of paragraph 30 of Annex VII to that agreement.

items.description: A person who is a qualifying borrower, and who makes a payment of relevant loan interest to a qualifying lender, is entitled to pay that interest under deduction of tax under the mortgage interest relief at source scheme (MIRAS).

The National Health Service Trusts (Consultation on Dissolution) (Scotland) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the consultation to be completed before the Secretary of State may make an order dissolving an NHS trust under paragraph 25 of Schedule 7A to the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978. The Regulations also prescribe the consultation which must be carried out under paragraph 26 of Schedule 7A before the Secretary of State may make an order transferring any property, rights or liabilities of a trust which has been dissolved. Provision is made for consultation under paragraphs 25 and 26 to be combined with the consultation required under the National Health Service (Consultation before Establishment) (Scotland) Regulations 1991 before an NHS trust is established.

The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1993
items.description: Under Part III of Schedule 12 to the Local Government Finance Act 1992, Scottish levying authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to the Secretary of State. Payments in respect of a provisional amount of the contribution are made during the financial year, final calculations and payments being made after the year ends. These Regulations amend the rules for the calculation of payments contained in the Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (Scotland) Regulations 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”).

The New Town (Irvine) Winding Up Order 1993

items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the exercise by the new governing body of a grant–maintained school of their powers before the implementation date of proposals for the acquisition of grant–maintained status.

items.title: The Education (Grant–maintained Schools) (Loans) Regulations 1993
items.description: These Regulations specify the classes or descriptions of expenditure in respect of which the appropriate authority may make loans to the governing bodies of grant–maintained schools under section 92 of the Education Act 1993 and the purposes for which, the terms upon which, and the manner in which such loans may be made.
